Posted: 16 March, 2025 Counter-Strike 2: Not an Update, a Rebirth At first glance, you think — oh, it's just CS:GO with ray tracing and protein shakes. Then you play it. And you realize: nope, this isn’t an update. It’s like your good old AK-47 suddenly developed a sense of smell.  CS2 is still that same adrenaline-fueled classic where one bullet can change the round, but now everything feels tangible. Source 2 didn’t just slap on better graphics — it poured a bucket of raw concrete into the game. You can feel things now: water droplets from explosions, the flicker of your teammate’s shadow around the corner, and smoke that doesn’t just exist — it lives.  Speaking of smoke — it’s no longer a wall. It’s a character. It moves, it breathes, it reacts to grenades like it’s got emotions. I wouldn’t be surprised if it starts streaming on Twitch next year.  The guns? Still the same arsenal, but every reload feels like you’re reloading your soul. The AWP doesn’t just boom anymore — it tears a hole in reality, pauses time, and gently lets you back into the match.  Servers finally hit 128-tick, and that’s like upgrading from smoke signals to quantum teleportation. Every movement is a dance. Every kill? Poetry. The feel is tighter, snappier, and your muscle memory comes back like a loyal dog that never really left.  It’s not perfect — hitboxes sometimes act like confused teenagers, and the UI is a bit extra. Matchmaking still throws you in with people who think the bomb site is a Spotify playlist. But that’s fine. CS2 is a foundation, not a fad. It's here to evolve.  The Verdict: CS2 isn’t the second chapter. It’s a second life. For the aim gods and the eco warriors. For the smurfs, the noobs, the spray control philosophers. It’s not just a game — it’s the art of control, the sound of chaos, and the silence before the last shot.  9.5/10 — Half a point off because there’s still no button that says, “Call mom, I clutched again.”

Posted: 10 December, 2020 After 8 years playing it, I didn't improve my skills in-game.  However, I learned new language skills: now I can curse in Russian and Brazilian Portuguese.  Also, my tolerance level to guttural screams and loud sounds was increased.  Now I can bear the crying of my children for longer and I can make them feel better by singing sweet traditional songs from other cultures, such as Очи чёрные (Dark Eyes) or Garota de Ipanema (The Girl from Ipanema).  In conclusion, Counter Strike transformed me into a better father and a better person, in addition to making me more patient, tolerant to hateful people and more trained in languages and cultures.  Better than any psychiatric therapy. 10/10

Posted: February 15 I installed Counter-Strike 2 thinking I was going to have fun.  What I got instead was a highly advanced psychological experiment conducted by Valve to measure how long five strangers can blame each other before someone rage-quits.  The game promises:  “Sub-tick precision”  “Enhanced graphics”  “Improved smoke physics”  What it actually delivers:  Getting one-tapped by someone who appears to be pre-aiming your future grandchildren  Teammates who bought a Negev on pistol round because “trust me bro”  Smokes so realistic you can’t see anything... including your will to win  The ranking system is a masterpiece. You can top frag, entry, clutch twice, plant the bomb, defuse the bomb, solve global warming and still lose 480 rating because your teammate decided to disconnect after round three to “fix his mic.”  Every match follows the same five stages:  Optimism  Confusion  Blame  Copium  “One more game”  Hit registration feels like you’re politely asking the enemy if they’d consider taking damage. Meanwhile, they shoot in your general postcode and you’re instantly back in spectator mode.  And yet… somehow… you queue again.  10/10 worst experience of my life. See you tomorrow.

Posted: March 3, 2017 "Teamwork makes the dream work" — or makes you question humanity  Review: After 1500+ hours of gameplay, I’ve unlocked a deep understanding of teamwork—or so I thought, until my teammate flashbanged the entire team into oblivion, planted the bomb on the wrong site, and then called us noobs. This game isn’t just a tactical shooter; it’s a masterclass in trust issues, skill issues, and every other kind of nonsense issue you didn’t know you had.  Here are a few invaluable lessons I’ve picked up along the way: The difference between a teammate and an enemy? Just the color of their uniform—and yes, I’ll still shoot both.  Communication is key. Unfortunately, when your team speaks six different languages, “Rush B” turns into a sitcom horror show.  90% of the game is tactical planning. The other 10% is peeking a corner and getting headshot faster than you can say “AWP.”  There’s no greater thrill than defusing the bomb with 0.01 seconds left on the clock—except realizing your teammate had a defuse kit the entire time and was watching you struggle.    This isn’t just a game; it’s a journey of through pain and chaos. Every round feels like a masterclass in tactical genius—until someone decides to buy and play The maps are intricate mazes designed to teach you how many angles you didn’t check before dying.  feels like conquering Mount Everest. feels like slipping on a banana peel while holding a Either way, you’ll scream, and    The visuals are and but the are the I now own a digital knife worth more than my rent, and I couldn’t be prouder.  The sound design is so it’s borderline Every footstep makes you every gunshot makes you and every gives you to that one time you peeked mid and    The players are a mix of tactical prodigies, comedy geniuses, and people who just discovered their keyboard has a jump key. One game, you’re part of a The next, your teammate is crouch-walking into a wall while blasting in voice chat.           This game will test your patience, your reflexes, and your will to live. It’s a rollercoaster of epic clutches, humiliating losses, and moments so absurd you’ll laugh until you cry. Somehow, despite the chaos, you’ll keep coming back for  If you enjoy balancing frustration, adrenaline, and moments of pure comedy, this game is your new best friend—and worst enemy. Just remember: it’s not a game—it’s a lifestyle, a therapy session, and a financial investment all in one.  – “Rush B, cyka blyat!”

Posted: February 5, 2024 Product received for free  Counter-Strike 2: When the Dream of Becoming a Pro Player Came Closer!  Hello, gamers! Today we'll be talking about Counter-Strike 2.  Remember the day you first opened CS 1.6? When every shot seemed fateful, and a victory over a teammate was a reason to celebrate for a week? That's when your love for this legendary discipline was born. But now Valve has decided to take the next step and release a new version of this beloved game.  CS2 retains the spirit of the good old classic, but added so many cool features that even veterans are wondering, "Maybe it's time to go back?" Now the maps look even more realistic, the bullet physics have changed so much that you'll have to relearn how to land headshots, and smoke grenades are so dense, it's as if the developers themselves were playing against cheaters.  By the way, did you know that the first CS tournament took place back in 2000? Back then, the prize pool was only a couple thousand dollars, but today, the best teams receive millions of dollars for winning majors like PGL Major Stockholm or BLAST Premier World Finals.  Is it realistic to break into the pro scene?  Every newcomer dreams of one day stepping into the big arena and hearing the roar of the crowd. And that's where CS2 gives us our chance! Thanks to new anti-cheat technologies and improved server stability, the chances of climbing the career ladder have increased significantly. The main thing is to practice, watch streams of famous players like s1mple or ZywOo, and believe in yourself! Although now it's better to hone your skills on the FACEIT platform.  But let's be honest: the path to the top is thorny. First, your teammates will blame you ("You missed again!"), then you'll have microphone issues ("I can't hear you at all"), and of course, the eternal classic—lag in the deciding round ("Why did I get killed through the wall?!"). But what a thrill it is when you finally manage to assemble your dream team and take first place at a local tournament!  So yes, my friends, CS2 isn't just a game, it's a lifetime, full of adrenaline, emotions, and unforgettable moments. So grab your mouse, put on your headphones, and get ready to conquer the heights of the esports scene with us!  P.S.: Don't forget to record a demo of your most epic moment—it'll come in handy for highlights!

Posted: March 24, 2024 Very long time player here and so far all I can say is Valve is majorly dropping the ball. If any other AAA or large company had a game with this many flaws and major problems they would be getting lambasted on every gaming outlet and forum across the internet. Valves DM servers are almost entirely controlled by botted accounts meant to farm cases/farm hours to be sold. The server browser forces you to tab out and use a browser that looks about 12 years out of date. Oh did I mention the browser is dominated by faked player servers almost all of which are based in Russia and show up no matter what your search parameters are set to? Comp splits your rank to every single map and moves up at a snails pace. Most maps that I have nearly 100% winrate on with 30+ games played on that map and I'm barely above halfway in the rank tier. The new ranked mode (Premier) is infested and owned by cheaters entirely at this point. Starting at 15,000 rating you start encountering brand new accounts who don't even try and hide cheats. 25,000+ is 5v5 Hack v Hack. These cheater matches dont even involve kills that aren't wallbangs. Valves own servers are owned entirely by cheaters at this point. Anyone legit looking to play even at a casual level and up to any tier of competitive is forced to play Faceit a third party service with an actual functioning anti-cheat.  Not only is CS2 a failure so far but they didn't even have the decency to keep up support for valve CSGO servers. Shameful. Game needs a massive turn around if I'm ever gonna take it seriously. Just another massive failure and disappointment in an already disappointing field of entertainment.  What an embarassment.  REVIEW UPDATE The last vac wave seems to have cleaned up the rankings a bit and it seems to be sticking around at least for now. I've managed to achieve 27k rating in NA. That being said there is still an infrequent amount of pretty obvious closet cheaters and the occasional rage cheater. Rank #1 world player is still cheating (he has been since the beta) for example.  Game still has ongoing issues that haven't been addressed or at least still not fully fixed as we approach the year anniversary. Headboosting is still buggy. Rubberbanding when bumping teammates still presents itself. The mini "teleport jumps" as you tag/get tagged by enemies is still very jarring. Smoke/molly extinguishing can still be a crapshoot at times. Moving up and down ramps still moves your crosshair vertically in a very jarring and unecessary way. Plenty of various animation bugs with player models especially in relation to ramp movements. These are just some of the things I can mention off of the top of my head. There is still a kill feedback delay that wasn't present in CSGO that makes the game feel slower or just clunky and unpolished.  Besides the gameplay problems we still dont have retakes, danger zone, flying scoutsman, etc etc. The server browser is still a piece of crap that takes you out of the game and it is STILL infested with faked servers. WTF valve how can you not fix this.  Oh yeah and the greatest tragedy of all. Surf is still broken and dead in the water for CS2  But don't worry at the end of the day the in game microtransaction and gambling features will always be working no matter what.

Posted: October 1, 2023 I've been a long time player of counterstrike. I was known as Stumpsy or sTuMpS, I was born with a disability (no hands) and I stumbled across CSGO around 2013. I was obviously really bad when I first started and only played CS as a casual game with friends who were much better than me. It wasn't until I started messing around with mouse/keyboard settings that changed everything for me. I discovered the setting Raw Mouse Input on/off and for me, not being able to pick up the mouse with no fingers, what this setting did literally changed the way I was able to play and literally accelerated gameplay for me. If I flicked fast in a direction I was able to navigate around maps and aim without having to lift my mouse off the pad. So say if I was turning right it would be a fast flick to right, back to center, fast flick to right again etc. Doing this I could basically do a full 360 within like 3-4 swipes. If i had raw mouse input turned on, that setting negates as it only follows where the mouse moves. It really bummed me out that removing this setting has pretty much ended my entire csgo gaming experience and considering they deleted csgo altogether with the replacement of CS2. I was looking forward to playing the game with friends and I've tried so many different setting to try and replicate what raw mouse input did for me. This game was the only fps game that offered a setting like that and its the only reason I stuck around playing csgo for so many years. It sucks watching all your friends play without you and it sucks feeling like you can no longer enjoy a game that gave you so much joy for so many years. I hope the devs at Valve see this and add the setting back into the game.  I have included a video explaining how I play and why mouse raw is so important for me.    and another video of my twitch compilations to show off actual gameplay.

Posted: May 2, 2020 Product received for free  No wonder CS is 20+ years old. This game is so addictive. I was never really into gaming but this game changed my opinion. This is one of the most accurate FPS games ever made with 128 tick servers. This game requires passion, dedication, hardwork, skill & a lot of practice because every pixel counts when you trigger you gun. Rumours say CS:GO is going to shift on Source 2 Engine within 2 weeks, really excited for it.  When you will play on low tier like Silver & Gold Nova you may lose many games because of your teammates. They may do basic mistakes like Run & Gun, Team Flash etc. But never rage on them. Always remember we used to do the same basic mistakes when we were beginners. I know this game involves lot of emotion but keep your calm & make sure to teach the beginners. Our community is already toxic, lets not make it more.  You win or you learn, you never lose.    Edit: Source 2 is still not out. You can play on 128 tick rate servers on third party clients like FaceIt, ESEA, 5E Play etc.

Posted: April 17, 2024 After 2 years playing it, I didn't improve my skills in-game.  However, I learned new language skills: now I can curse in Russian and Brazilian Portuguese.  Also, my tolerance level to guttural screams and loud sounds was increased.  Now I can bear the crying of my children for longer and I can make them feel better by singing sweet traditional songs from other cultures, such as Очи чёрные (Dark Eyes) or Garota de Ipanema (The Girl from Ipanema).  In conclusion, Counter Strike transformed me into a better father and a better person, in addition to making me more patient, tolerant to hateful people and more trained in languages and cultures.  Better than any psychiatric therapy. 10/10
